channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Who will — and won't — be guests at the State of the Union address
item.description: Invitees at Tuesday night's address by President Trump include the gold-medalist men's U.S. hockey team, while Democratic lawmakers have invited several Epstein survivors.
item.content:encoded:
Invitees at Tuesday night's address by President Trump include the gold-medalist men's U.S. hockey team, while Democratic lawmakers have invited several Epstein survivors.

(Image credit: Nathan Howard)
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie

item.title: Read NPR's annotated fact check of President Trump's State of the Union
item.description: As President Trump delivers his State of the Union address, reporters from across NPR's newsroom will fact check his speech and offer context.
item.content:encoded:
As President Trump delivers his State of the Union address, reporters from across NPR's newsroom will fact check his speech and offer context.

(Image credit: Brendan Smialowski)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: President Trump delivers first State of the Union address of his second term
item.description: Facing low approval ratings and ahead of midterm elections in November, President Trump delivers the first State of the Union address of his second term as president Tuesday night.
item.content:encoded:
Facing low approval ratings and ahead of midterm elections in November, President Trump delivers the first State of the Union address of his second term as president Tuesday night.

(Image credit: Andrew Caballero-Reynolds)
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: U.S. House rejects aviation safety bill after Pentagon abruptly withdraws support
item.description: The House of Representatives narrowly rejected a bipartisan aviation safety bill that was spurred by the deadly midair collision near Washington, D.C., after the Pentagon abruptly withdrew support.
item.content:encoded:
The House of Representatives narrowly rejected a bipartisan aviation safety bill that was spurred by the deadly midair collision near Washington, D.C., after the Pentagon abruptly withdrew support.

(Image credit: Mariam Zuhaib)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: China and the U.S. alter foreign aid strategies
item.description: China's foreign aid strategy has shifted in the last few decades Now its model may be the one the U.S. is adopting even as China moves away from it.
item.content:encoded:
China's foreign aid strategy has shifted in the last few decades Now its model may be the one the U.S. is adopting even as China moves away from it.

(Image credit: Chen Yehua/Xinhua via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: Flavor Flav is among women's hockey team fans outraged by presidential snub
item.description: The rapper, who also serves as the official "hype man" for multiple U.S. Olympic teams, invited the female hockey players to Las Vegas for a "real celebration."
item.content:encoded:
The rapper, who also serves as the official "hype man" for multiple U.S. Olympic teams, invited the female hockey players to Las Vegas for a "real celebration."

(Image credit: Andrew Milligan)
item.dc:creator: Neda Ulaby

item.title: Hegseth threatens to blacklist Anthropic over 'woke AI' concerns
item.description: The company's Claude chatbot is one of the few AI systems cleared for use in classified settings. But a standoff between Anthropic and the Trump administration is putting its government work at risk.
item.content:encoded:
The company's Claude chatbot is one of the few AI systems cleared for use in classified settings. But a standoff between Anthropic and the Trump administration is putting its government work at risk.

(Image credit: STAFF)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Listen to NPR live coverage of the State of the Union
item.description: President Trump will address the nation Tuesday night to outline his vision and priorities for his administration's second year. Listen to NPR's live special coverage of the speech and the Democratic response.
item.content:encoded: President Trump will address the nation Tuesday night to outline his vision and priorities for his administration's second year. Listen to NPR's live special coverage of the speech and the Democratic response.
item.dc:creator: Heidi Glenn

item.title: The Louvre Museum's director has resigned in the wake of October's brazen jewel heist
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron accepted Laurence des Cars' resignation as "an act of responsibility" at a moment when the Louvre needs security upgrades, modernization and other major projects.
item.content:encoded:
French President Emmanuel Macron accepted Laurence des Cars' resignation as "an act of responsibility" at a moment when the Louvre needs security upgrades, modernization and other major projects.

(Image credit: Emma Da Silva)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Gene therapy is transforming lives, but for many Americans it's hard to reach
item.description: Gene therapies have the potential to cure some diseases, but they are extraordinarily expensive. Location can also be a big hurdle for patients seeking this specialized care.
item.content:encoded:
Gene therapies have the potential to cure some diseases, but they are extraordinarily expensive. Location can also be a big hurdle for patients seeking this specialized care.

(Image credit: Annie Flanagan for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin
